CERT-001.name: Certificate templates (configuration partition listing)
CERT-001.description: Full LDAP listing of certificate template objects. Use for inventory and to scope ADCS-ESC reviews. Each row is a PKI object, not proof of vulnerability by itself.
CERT-001.remediation: Use results for inventory and change tracking. Prioritize templates with client-auth EKU and weak enrollment ACLs using ADCS-ESC1–ESC8.

CERT-006.description: Certificate templates that include client authentication EKU (1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.2). These are common targets for domain authentication abuse when combined with weak enrollment or subject/SAN settings.
CERT-006.remediation: Restrict enrollment; require manager approval where appropriate; ensure subject/SAN flags and msPKI settings align with least privilege; cross-check ADCS-ESC1–ESC3.

CERT-007.description: Templates granting smart card logon EKU (1.3.6.1.4.1.311.20.2.2). Mis-issued smart card logon certs can support credential abuse paths; review issuance and physical controls.
CERT-007.remediation: Limit enrollment; validate key archival and issuance policies; ensure smart card issuance aligns with tier model.

CERT-008.description: Templates with PKINIT client authentication EKU (1.3.6.1.5.2.3.4). Certificates can be used in Kerberos PKINIT flows; combined with weak enrollment they raise domain authentication risk.
CERT-008.remediation: Tighten enrollment ACLs; review PKINIT usage; validate KDC and domain controller certificate binding settings.

CERT-009.description: Templates allowing code signing EKU (1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.3). Abuse can support malware signing or policy bypass if enrollment is too broad.
CERT-009.remediation: Restrict code signing template enrollment to designated groups; audit issued certificates.

CERT-010.description: Templates without manager approval (CT_FLAG_PEND_ALL_REQUESTS not set in msPKI-Enrollment-Flag). Speeds issuance and enlarges the window for mis-issued certificates when other controls fail.
CERT-010.remediation: Enable manager approval for sensitive templates; combine with restricted enrollment and ADCS-ESC reviews.

CERT-011.description: Templates with zero or missing required RA signatures (msPKI-RA-Signature). Weakens issuance workflow and can ease abuse when enrollment is misconfigured.
CERT-011.remediation: Require RA signatures where policy demands; align with PKI officer workflows.

CERT-012.description: Enterprise CA enrollment service objects (pKIEnrollmentService) in AD. Lists published CAs and template bindings; use for mapping attack surface alongside ADCS-ESC6–ESC8.
CERT-012.remediation: Validate CA host hardening, Web Enrollment, and RPC exposure; review certificateTemplates published per CA.

CERT-013.description: NTAuthCertificates store in AD lists CAs trusted for domain authentication. Unexpected or stale entries can affect trust decisions.
CERT-013.remediation: Keep NTAuth aligned with intentional enterprise roots; remove retired CAs after migration.

CERT-014.description: Certification authority objects under the enterprise PKI tree. Broad listing helps identify roots and intermediates published to the forest.
CERT-014.remediation: Audit CA certificates for lifecycle and trust alignment; remove obsolete publishing.

CERT-015.description: Templates missing pKIExpirationPeriod (or equivalent validity constraints visible in LDAP). May indicate legacy or inconsistent template definitions worth validating in CA policy.
CERT-015.remediation: Define and enforce validity periods per template; validate on issuing CA.

CERT-016.description: Templates with key archival enrollment flag set. Increases sensitivity: archived keys require strict access controls on KRA and CA database.
CERT-016.remediation: Verify KRA configuration, ACLs, and auditing; limit templates with archival to required scenarios.

CERT-017.description: Templates allowing exportable private keys (msPKI-Private-Key-Flag). Eases credential theft if certificates are issued to interactive users or broadly enrolled.
CERT-017.remediation: Disable exportable keys where not required; prefer hardware protection for high-value use cases.

CERT-018.description: Templates with machine/enrollment name flags indicating machine certificate scenarios. Review for unintended domain computer authentication surface.
CERT-018.remediation: Restrict enrollment to intended device OUs and groups; validate autoenrollment GPO scope.

CERT-019.description: Templates allowing renewal with a new key pair. Can complicate revocation and lifecycle tracking if combined with weak identity binding.
CERT-019.remediation: Ensure renewal policies and revocation workflows cover key-change renewals.

CERT-023.description: User accounts with userCertificate populated. Published certs may support mapping or smart card scenarios; unexpected entries warrant review for stale or over-published credentials.
CERT-023.remediation: Validate intentional publication; remove obsolete certificates from the directory.

CERT-024.description: Computer accounts with userCertificate populated. May reflect domain controller or service certs published to AD; unexpected hosts warrant validation.
CERT-024.remediation: Confirm published certs match intended roles; rotate and clean up stale entries.

CERT-025.description: CRL distribution point objects in the configuration partition. Use to map revocation publication points and HTTP/S surface.
CERT-025.remediation: Ensure CDP URLs are reachable and HTTPS where required; monitor expiration and replication.

CERT-026.name: [DACL review] Certificate templates (nTSecurityDescriptor for enrollment ACL analysis)
CERT-026.description: Lists templates with security descriptor loaded. LDAP cannot resolve 'Everyone' enrollment in one filter—manually review DACL for Authenticated Users / Domain Users enrollment. Prefer ADCS-ESC4 for write-equivalent abuse on templates.
CERT-026.remediation: Audit enrollment ACEs on each template; remove broad enrollment where inappropriate; correlate with ADCS-ESC4.

CERT-027.description: CA objects referencing offline roots or cross-boundary trust material. Use for PKI topology documentation and continuity planning.
CERT-027.remediation: Validate CRL/AIA publication for offline roots; document trust chains.

CERT-028.description: AIA container under Public Key Services. Authority Information Access publication affects chain building for clients.
CERT-028.remediation: Ensure AIA URLs are correct, reachable, and aligned with issuance hierarchy.

CERT-029.description: Templates with subject name from Active Directory (CT_FLAG_SUBJECT_ALT_REQUIRE_UPN or related name flags in msPKI-Certificate-Name-Flag). Affects how subjects are bound to directory identities.
CERT-029.remediation: Validate name flags against intended use; cross-check with ESC1-style SAN issues.

CERT-030.name: [Listing] Template schema version fields (all templates)
CERT-030.description: Lists all templates with schema/minor revision attributes. Useful for spotting legacy versions; not a vulnerability by itself.
CERT-030.remediation: Deprecate legacy templates; migrate to supported schema versions with explicit EKU and ACLs.

CERT-031.name: [Listing] Certificate templates (full forest set; manual ESC review)
CERT-031.description: Broad listing only—same objects as a full template inventory. Use to drive manual review; does not imply vulnerable configuration without ADCS-ESC and ACL analysis.
CERT-031.remediation: Cross-reference with ADCS-ESC1–ESC8 and per-template ACL review; retire unused templates.

CERT-032.name: [Listing] Templates for enrollment rights review (full set)
CERT-032.description: Lists every template with timestamps—LDAP does not enumerate enrollment ACEs in this filter. Use for workflow tracking; validate enrollment with ADCS-ESC4 and DACL review.
CERT-032.remediation: Verify only intended groups may enroll; remove excessive ACEs.

CERT-033.name: Client authentication templates (same LDAP scope as CERT-006)
CERT-033.description: Intentionally the same filter as CERT-006 (client authentication EKU). Retained for checklist parity; findings overlap—do not double-count risk. Scoring suppressed.
CERT-033.remediation: Use CERT-006 or ADCS-ESC checks for reporting; disable one ID in overrides if duplicates are undesirable.

CERT-034.description: Enterprise CA objects with timestamps for health-style review. LDAP alone does not prove outage; use with monitoring and certutil/CA logs.
CERT-034.remediation: Monitor CA services, CRL validity, and disk space on CA hosts.

CERT-035.description: CRL distribution points with change metadata. Supports tracking revocation publication changes.
CERT-035.remediation: Validate CRL publication schedules and HTTP/S endpoints.

CERT-036.description: Enterprise CA objects relevant to OCSP deployment review. LDAP does not prove OCSP responder health—follow with endpoint tests.
CERT-036.remediation: Deploy and monitor OCSP responders per Microsoft guidance; align AIA/OCSP URLs.

CERT-037.name: [Listing] Templates for version drift review (full set)
CERT-037.description: Lists all templates—LDAP does not detect 'mismatch' without comparing to published CA policy. Manual review only.

CERT-038.name: [Listing] Templates for autoenrollment policy alignment (full set)
CERT-038.description: Broad template listing for GPO/autoenrollment correlation. LDAP alone does not read autoenrollment GPOs.
CERT-038.remediation: Scope autoenrollment GPOs tightly; review templates granted autoenrollment.

CERT-039.name: Templates with Certificate Request Agent EKU
CERT-039.description: Templates publishing the CRA EKU (1.3.6.1.4.1.311.20.2.1). Overlaps ADCS-ESC3—validate enrollment and manager approval.
CERT-039.remediation: Remove CRA EKU where unnecessary; enforce manager approval and enrollment restrictions.

CERT-040.description: Enterprise CA objects tied to Web Enrollment attack surface. Correlates with ADCS-ESC8 for HTTP/NTLM exposure—this LDAP check does not replace network verification.
CERT-040.remediation: Require HTTPS, harden IIS certsrv, restrict NTLM; see KB5005413.

CERT-041.name: Templates with supersession reference (msPKI-Template-Supersede)
CERT-041.description: Templates that reference another template in the supersession chain. Validate whether superseded templates are disabled where policy requires.
CERT-041.remediation: Disable superseded templates after migration; enforce supersession links.

CERT-042.description: Enterprise CAs for CA certificate lifetime review. Expired or soon-to-expire CA certs break chain validation.
CERT-042.remediation: Plan CA cert renewal; monitor AIA/CRL publication during rollover.

CERT-043.name: Templates with minimal key size at or below 1024 bits (LDAP)
CERT-043.description: Templates advertising msPKI-Minimal-Key-Size ≤ 1024. Modern baselines typically require ≥2048; confirm algorithm context on issuing CA.
CERT-043.remediation: Raise minimum key size on templates and CAs; reissue where non-compliant.

CERT-044.name: [Listing] Template objects for validity period review (LDAP cannot compare periods)
CERT-044.description: Broad listing: LDAP does not decode pKIExpirationPeriod for numeric comparison in this check. Manual or CA-policy review required for 'excessive' validity.
CERT-044.remediation: Reduce validity periods to operational minimums; enforce renewal discipline.

CERT-045.description: Enterprise CAs for audit configuration review. LDAP listing does not read local CA audit policy—use CA host baselines.
CERT-045.remediation: Enable and forward CA audit logs; align with SIEM coverage.

CERT-046.name: [DACL review] Templates with security descriptor (ACL triage)
CERT-046.description: Loads nTSecurityDescriptor for manual or downstream ACL review. Prefer ADCS-ESC4 for automated weak-ACL detection.
CERT-046.remediation: Remove write-equivalent rights for non-PKI admins; validate ADCS-ESC4 output.

CERT-047.description: Enterprise CAs for backup posture review. AD LDAP does not prove backup success—follow with backup reports.
CERT-047.remediation: Back up CA database and keys per Microsoft DR guidance; test restore.

CERT-048.description: Enterprise CAs for revocation workflow documentation. Pair with operational CRL/OCSP monitoring.
CERT-048.remediation: Maintain revocation reason policies and responder availability.

CERT-049.name: [Listing] Smart card logon template review (full set; see CERT-007 for EKU-specific)
CERT-049.description: Legacy stub used a broad template list. For EKU-specific smart card templates use CERT-007. This row is listing-oriented; scoring suppressed.
CERT-049.remediation: Validate smart card templates, KDC certs, and user mappings.

CERT-050.name: Templates with Any Purpose or missing EKU (ESC2-style LDAP signal)
CERT-050.description: Templates with Any Purpose EKU or no EKU—high-risk configuration class. Overlaps ADCS-ESC2; use ESC2 for full logic.
CERT-050.remediation: Explicitly set required EKUs; remove Any Purpose where not justified.

CERT-051.description: Enterprise CAs for CA role separation review. Combined CA admin + backup operator on same accounts increases insider risk.
CERT-051.remediation: Separate CA administration per Microsoft PKI best practices.

CERT-052.name: [Listing] Subject name policy review (all templates)
CERT-052.description: Broad listing for subject/SAN flag review. Prefer targeted checks (e.g. CERT-029, ADCS-ESC1) for specific misconfigurations.
CERT-052.remediation: Tighten subject name flags; disable enrollee-supplied subject where not needed.

CERT-053.description: Enterprise CA objects for security descriptor review. Weak ACLs on pKIEnrollmentService relate to ADCS-ESC7—use LDAP plus ESC7 for full analysis.
CERT-053.remediation: Restrict ManageCA and related rights to PKI admins; validate with ADCS-ESC7.
